overall_score.method: Weighted average of pledge status: backed=100, partial=50, mismatch=0, unknown=25. Excludes pledges dependent on Westminster legislation.

pledges.title: Pass an Integrated Education Act
pledges.evidence: Integrated Education Act (NI) 2022 passed — Alliance private member's bill, cross-community support.

pledges.title: Legislate for a 30-year NI Climate Action Plan
pledges.evidence: Climate Change Act (NI) 2022 enacted, committing to net zero by 2050 with interim 2030 and 2040 targets.

pledges.title: Reform the planning system to unblock housing
pledges.evidence: Planning (Amendment) Act 2016 updates implemented, but major reform bill stalled in committee as of Q1 2026.

pledges.title: Invest £1bn in mental health services over the mandate
pledges.evidence: £600m committed in 2022-25 Programme for Government. Full £1bn target pending final two years of mandate.

pledges.title: Pass a standalone Hate Crime Bill
pledges.evidence: Criminal Justice (Aggravated Offences) Bill introduced 2024, second reading passed. Committee stage ongoing.

pledges.title: Abolish academic selection at 11 within the mandate
pledges.evidence: No legislation introduced. DUP and UUP opposition blocked Executive agreement. Selection tests continue as of 2026.

pledges.title: Deliver a social care reform bill
pledges.evidence: Social Care Strategy published 2023. Primary legislation not yet introduced as of Q1 2026.

pledges.title: Establish a Commissioner for Older People
pledges.evidence: Commissioner for Older People Act (NI) 2011 commissioner re-appointed with extended remit 2023.

pledges.title: Introduce a domestic rating reform
pledges.evidence: Rates reform Green Paper published 2024 but no legislation introduced. Blocked by Executive disagreement.

pledges.title: Deliver a renewable energy strategy for NI
pledges.evidence: NI Energy Strategy 2021-2030 adopted. Offshore wind targets set. Grid upgrade investment confirmed.

pledges.title: Tackle public sector waiting lists through new investment
pledges.evidence: Waiting list task force established 2023. Waiting times reduced by 8% in highest-pressure areas but overall list remains at record levels.

pledges.title: Pass an Anti-Poverty Act with statutory targets
pledges.evidence: Executive agreed to a Child Poverty Strategy but no statutory targets enacted. Draft strategy only as of 2026.

overall_score.method: Weighted average of pledge status. Score significantly reduced by 22-month Stormont boycott (Feb 2022–Jan 2024) which prevented delivery of programme for government commitments.

pledges.title: Remove the Irish Sea border through the Protocol
pledges.evidence: Windsor Framework (2023) reduced checks on retail goods. DUP accepted as partial resolution; core checks on food and agricultural goods remain, triggering resumed boycott concerns.

pledges.title: Protect grammar schools and academic selection
pledges.evidence: No legislation abolishing selection passed. Grammar schools fully operational. Transfer tests conducted in 2023, 2024, 2025.

pledges.title: Oppose any movement toward an Irish Language Act
pledges.evidence: Identity and Language (NI) Act 2022 passed at Westminster despite DUP opposition, establishing an Irish Language Commissioner.

pledges.title: Reduce waiting lists through additional health investment
pledges.evidence: 22-month Stormont boycott meant no executive was formed to agree health budgets. Waiting lists rose to record highs during boycott period.

pledges.title: Secure devolved corporation tax powers
pledges.evidence: Fiscal framework agreed 2023. Corporation tax devolution not yet activated — awaiting financial sustainability confirmation.

pledges.title: Reform the Civic Forum to give unionism a stronger voice
pledges.evidence: Civic Forum reform has not been progressed. No legislation or consultation published as of 2026.

pledges.title: Pass a legacy bill that protects veterans
pledges.evidence: Legacy Act 2023 passed at Westminster. DUP supported veteran immunity provisions but opposed some aspects of the information retrieval mechanism.

pledges.title: Deliver a rural proofing strategy for all Executive decisions
pledges.evidence: Rural proofing guidance existed but no new statutory strategy produced during mandate period. Boycott disrupted delivery.

pledges.title: Cap rates bills for small businesses
pledges.evidence: Small Business Rate Relief extended annually 2022-2026. Transitional relief introduced for hospitality sector.

pledges.title: Expand infrastructure investment in Greater Belfast
pledges.evidence: York Street interchange and A5 road projects delayed. No additional commitments during boycott. Infrastructure budget cut in 2024-25.

pledges.title: Protect the NHS from any all-island health structures
pledges.evidence: No all-island health integration measures enacted. DUP used ministerial veto powers to block cross-border health referral proposals.

pledges.title: Oppose gender recognition reform and related legislation
pledges.evidence: Gender Recognition Reform blocked from extending to NI. DUP vote and Westminster legislation both opposed extension.

overall_score.method: Weighted average of pledge status. Score reflects partial delivery on healthcare and housing under constrained Executive budgets.

pledges.title: Secure an Irish Language Act
pledges.evidence: Identity and Language (NI) Act 2022 passed at Westminster — Irish Language Commissioner, Ulster Scots Commissioner, and NSMC language body established.

pledges.title: Deliver a ten-year plan to clear healthcare waiting lists
pledges.evidence: Elective Care Framework published. Waiting lists fell 8% in cardiology and orthopaedics 2024-25 but overall unscheduled care lists rose.

pledges.title: Introduce a cost-of-living payment for low-income households
pledges.evidence: £150 cost-of-living payment made to 290,000 households in 2022-23. Supplementary payment of £100 in 2024.

pledges.title: Build 100,000 social and affordable homes by 2028
pledges.evidence: NI Housing Executive target revised to 18,000 new social homes 2022-27. 9,200 completed as of Q1 2026, on pace for 15,000.

pledges.title: Establish all-island data infrastructure and digital economy
pledges.evidence: InterTrade Ireland digital economy fund established. Full all-island data strategy not yet published.

pledges.title: Win a legacy mechanism that delivers truth for victims
pledges.evidence: Legacy Act 2023 passed against SF objections. Conditional immunity mechanism condemned by SF and victims groups. Judicial review ongoing.

pledges.title: Deliver free prescriptions for all in NI
pledges.evidence: Prescription charges abolished in NI since 2010; maintained throughout mandate. Extension to dental check-ups agreed 2024.

pledges.title: Introduce a homelessness prevention duty on councils
pledges.evidence: Housing Amendments Act (NI) 2024 introduces prevention duty — councils must assess risk within 56 days. Guidance published; implementation ongoing.

pledges.title: Develop a cross-border greenway network
pledges.evidence: Shared Island Fund committed €500m to cross-border greenways. Belfast-Dublin cycleway phase 1 complete. Phase 2 planning underway.

pledges.title: Protect welfare mitigation payments beyond 2022
pledges.evidence: Welfare Supplementary Payments Act (NI) renewed 2023. Mitigation payments totalling £85m per year continued.

pledges.title: Achieve a statutory Irish language census question
pledges.evidence: 2021 census did not include Irish language proficiency question. Next census due 2031. Lobbying ongoing.

pledges.title: Deliver a Border Poll within the mandate
pledges.evidence: No border poll called. Secretary of State has not met the legal threshold under the Good Friday Agreement to trigger one.

overall_score.method: Weighted average of pledge status. UUP in opposition for periods; score reflects influence rather than executive delivery.

pledges.title: Reform mental health provision with dedicated legislation
pledges.evidence: Mental Health Strategy 2021-31 adopted. Mental Capacity Act (NI) commenced 2023. Crisis care services expanded.

pledges.title: Protect grammar schools and parental choice in education
pledges.evidence: Academic selection retained. UUP voted against Alliance abolition motion. Grammar intake stable 2022-2026.

pledges.title: Deliver a clean air strategy and plastic reduction targets
pledges.evidence: Clean Air Strategy 2021 adopted. Single-use plastics ban implemented 2023 in line with UK rules.

pledges.title: Modernise the NI road network with a 10-year plan
pledges.evidence: 10-year infrastructure plan published 2023. A5 western corridor delayed by legal challenges. A1 upgrade proceeding.

pledges.title: Oppose the NI Protocol in its original form
pledges.evidence: UUP consistently voted against Protocol provisions and supported Windsor Framework as an improvement.

pledges.title: Deliver NHS reform to reduce agency spend
pledges.evidence: Agency spend fell 12% 2023-24 following nursing bursary reinstatement. Full transformation plan published but delayed by budget constraints.

pledges.title: Introduce a victims' legacy commission outside the courts
pledges.evidence: Legacy Act 2023 passed. UUP opposed the conditional immunity provisions as incompatible with ECHR. Judicial review supported by UUP.

pledges.title: Expand broadband across rural NI by 2025
pledges.evidence: Project Stratum delivered ultrafast broadband to 76,000 rural premises by 2025, but 11% of rural NI remains underserved.

pledges.title: Restore the NI Commissioner for Children's powers
pledges.evidence: Commissioner remit unchanged. No review published. UUP tabled questions but no legislative progress.

pledges.title: Champion NI's place in the United Kingdom
pledges.evidence: UUP consistently maintained unionist position in debate and legislation throughout mandate.

pledges.title: Protect the fishing industry from post-Brexit disruption
pledges.evidence: NI fishing industry received interim support payments 2022-23. Windsor Framework reduced some paperwork burden. Quota disputes unresolved.

pledges.title: Introduce a stalking protection act for NI
pledges.evidence: Stalking (NI) Act 2022 enacted. Stalking Protection Orders now available.

overall_score.method: Weighted average of pledge status. As an opposition party for much of the mandate, SDLP delivery scored primarily on voting record and private members' bills rather than Executive decisions.

pledges.title: Establish a dedicated poverty commission with statutory powers
pledges.evidence: Anti-Poverty Strategy 2022 adopted by Executive but without statutory targets. SDLP opposed the non-binding form.

pledges.title: Secure an SDLP MEP via a new EU relationship
pledges.evidence: UK left the EU in 2020. No MEP seats restored. NI Assembly participation in EU committees achieved via Windsor Framework but no voting rights.

pledges.title: Pass a domestic violence statutory framework
pledges.evidence: Domestic Abuse and Civil Proceedings Act (NI) 2021 already enacted. SDLP championed implementation and coercive control provisions activated 2022.

pledges.title: Establish community health and wellbeing hubs in every constituency
pledges.evidence: Integrated Care Partnerships established per Health and Social Care Board area (6 hubs). Not one per constituency (18).

pledges.title: Introduce a housing affordability index and price transparency
pledges.evidence: No housing affordability index legislation. SDLP raised in Finance Committee 2023 but no Executive response.

pledges.title: Strengthen community relations and interface management
pledges.evidence: PEACE PLUS programme £1.1bn 2021-27 operational. Interface regeneration fund extended. SDLP supported all measures.

pledges.title: Pass a Legacy Act that centres victims
pledges.evidence: SDLP opposed Legacy Act 2023 as prioritising state actors. SDLP supported the Irish Government legal challenge at the ECHR.

pledges.title: Deliver a free childcare entitlement for 2-year-olds
pledges.evidence: Childcare strategy published 2023. 15 hours per week for 3-4 year olds extended. 2-year-old entitlement not yet enacted.

pledges.title: Bring the Irish and British governments together on climate
pledges.evidence: NSMC environment sector met twice 2023. Joint climate communique issued but no binding cross-border targets.

pledges.title: Introduce mandatory Irish language standards in public services
pledges.evidence: Irish Language Commissioner established under Identity and Language Act 2022. Standards framework in development. Statutory guidance not yet published.

pledges.title: Establish a tenants' charter with enforcement powers
pledges.evidence: Private Tenancies Act (NI) 2022 strengthened deposit protection and notice periods. Full tenants' charter not enacted.

pledges.title: Reform agricultural support post-Brexit via a NI Agriculture Bill
pledges.evidence: Agriculture (Retained EU Law and Data) Act (NI) 2022 enacted. Full new policy framework deferred to 2026-27 mandate.
